element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Tutorial-like early sequences establish core mobility
Resource gathering established in opening (JP version: 20 coins given; US version: removed for difficulty)
Sustain mechanics introduced early (JP: big heart in 1-2; US: small heart, maximum heart in 1-3)
Three ghost boss characters with distinct patterns; US version increased health for difficulty spike
Spatial awareness required for safe passage
US version: enemies respawn at half-screen distance (increases tension); JP version: enemies stay dead (reduced pressure)

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating difficulty through level progression; US version employs enemy respawn system to maintain constant pressure; boss encounters serve as climactic tension peaks

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction with basic enemies and platforming; resource-rich (JP: 4 lives + 20 coins)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive enemy complexity and hazard density increases
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss encounters with increased health pools (especially US version); sustained pressure from respawning enemies
notable_spikes: Level 1 Boss: Three ghost characters with pattern-based combat
US version globally: Halved starting lives (2 vs 4) and removed coins create resource scarcity
US version Level 1 Boss: Increased boss health vs JP version

notable_level_patterns.description: Level 1 establishes core mechanics with manageable enemies and ample resources before introducing boss encounter
JP version emphasizes resource abundance and permanent enemy removal; US version employs respawning enemies and life/resource scarcity to increase challenge
Health item availability differs by region to modulate difficulty (JP: big heart in 1-2; US: small heart in 1-2, maximum heart in 1-3)

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Kid Dracula Game Boy article)
